title: hydroleaceae
intro: The Hydroleaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, specifically a small and diverse group of water-loving plants that are native to various regions around the world. Hydroleaceae includes around 70 species, commonly known as bladderworts and some species of Utricularia. These plants are typically found in aquatic or semi-aquatic environments, such as ponds, lakes, and wetlands, where they can absorb nutrients from the water. The Hydroleaceae family is characterized by its unique bladder-like traps, which are used to capture small organisms and provide essential nutrients.
